sections.paragraph:
fut. יִטֹּר and יִנְטֹור Jer 3:5 to guard, i.q. שָׁמַר; but mostly poet. (Syr. Chald. and Arab. نطر id. Kindred is נָצַר) e.g. to guard a vineyard, Son 1:6 8:11 , 12 .

(2) Specially to keep, sc. anger, which is understood, Psal. 103:9 , לֹא לְעֹולָם יִטֹּור “he will not keep (his anger) for ever,” Jerem. 3:5 , 12 followed by לְ of pers., Nah 1:2 אֵת Lev 19:18 compare שָׁמַר Jer 3:5 Job 10:14.

(3) i.q. Arab. نظر to keep watch (an idea derived from guarding); hence מַטָּרָה.
